action: SYSTEMIC: claimed inbox items strand forever in .evolve/inbox/processing/cycle-N/ because the promote-to-processed step died with legacy ship.sh (v12 flag day) and the go-native ship never inherited it. agents/evolve-triage.md Step 0 still documents the contract: 'File move is handled by Step 0a's claim call + ship.sh's post-commit promote' — the claim half works (inbox-mover claim), the promote half is GONE. Evidence: processing/ holds orphans from cycles 124, 265, 294, 295 (all shipped long ago), 304 and 306 (today); operator manually rescued the strands THREE times on 2026-06-12/13 (heuristic-gate-demotion twice, ledger-line-1740 twice). Effect: any item a cycle claims but does not finish becomes invisible to all future triage scans (Step 0a reads maxdepth-1 only) — committed work-specs silently evaporate; completed items never archive. Fix in the go-native postship path (go/internal/phases/ship/postship.go already reads triage-decision.json:91): after a successful cycle commit, for each claimed item in processing/<this-cycle>/ — if its id appears in triage-decision.json top_n[] AND the cycle shipped, move to processed/cycle-N/; otherwise move BACK to inbox root (unfinished claim = release the claim). Also handle the FAIL/abort terminal: cycle-end without ship must release all of processing/<cycle>/ back to inbox root. Backfill: sweep existing orphans (124/265/294/295) — completed ones to processed/, with a one-shot operator review list. Update the persona sentence to name the go-native owner. TDD pins: shipped-cycle completed item → processed/; failed-cycle claimed item → back to inbox root; double-claim race (file already moved) is a WARN not an error.
evidence_pointer: .evolve/inbox/processing/ orphans (cycles 124,265,294,295 + today's 304/306 rescues); agents/evolve-triage.md Step 0 note 7 (names deleted ship.sh); go/internal/phases/ship/postship.go
